column6: Asked or responded 5 or more times.
column9: Asked one question. Responded to two questions.
column12: Asked or responded to two questions.

column6: Provided high-quality math content in a thoughtful, fun, and meaningful way.
column9: Provided math-related suggestions and ideas in a meaningful and fun way.
column15: Provided responses that contained little math content.

column6: A full page of notes shown with multiple solved problems or diagrams.
column12: Less than 4 problems fully worked out or 3 or less problems partially worked out.

column6: Very neat writing. Organized logically. Labels and diagrams.
Deep understanding of concepts shown in accuracy.
column12: Some understanding. Shows some inaccuracy in multiple problems.

column6: All content is complete. Additional interesting and compelling content included.
column12: Most content or problems are complete

column6: Information is clear and concise with a professional look and feel.
column9: The structure of the content is very organized. It makes sense. Many labels are added to diagrams.
column12: Information was structured well. Some clarification about sections are needed.
column15: Somewhat organized. Diagrams contain few labels.
column18: Content is not organized and is very confusing and messy.

column6: No computational errors. Work demonstrates the process of being accurate.
Two excellent reviews given and received. Substantial revisions made.
column9: Two reviews given and received. Revisions made.
column12: One relevant review given and received with revisions. Or two without revisions.
column15: One relevant review given or received. No revisions made.
column18: Peer review has very little feedback and no revisions.

column6: Project content deeply integrates Biblical understanding thematically and scripturally. Builds on God's purpose in creation and human invention. Demonstrates how geometric concepts reveal God's character, design, and redemptive purposes. Connects mathematical beauty to worship and stewardship.
column9: Project includes meaningful Biblical connections with relevant Scripture. Discusses God's design in creation or human responsibility as image-bearers. Shows understanding of how geometry reflects God's order and purpose. Makes clear connections between math and faith.
column12: Project includes some Biblical references or Christian perspective. Mentions God's creation or design but lacks depth. Makes surface-level connections between geometry and faith without substantial development or scriptural support.
column15: Project includes one scriptural reference or minimal Christian perspective. Biblical connection feels added-on rather than integrated. Little evidence of thinking about geometry through a Biblical worldview or God's purposes in creation.
column18: Project lacks Biblical integration or Christian worldview. No scriptural references or discussion of God's design, creation purposes, or stewardship. Does not demonstrate understanding of geometry as revealing God's nature.

column6: Summary makes clear the scope, intent, and mathematical content. Project deeply integrates Biblical understanding thematically and scripturally.
column9: Summary clarifies scope, intent, and mathematical content. Includes Biblical integration idea.
column12: Summary provides two of the following: scope, intent, mathematical content, Biblical integration.
column15: Summary provides one of the following: scope, intent, mathematical content, Biblical integration.
column18: Project lacks Biblical integration or Christian worldview. No scriptural references or discussion of God's design, creation purposes, or stewardship. Does not demonstrate understanding of geometry as revealing God's nature.
